note: Hand-maintained, sourced key figures for the Australian AI industry end to end. Update values in place; keep source + as_of with every figure.

sections.title: Power & the grid
sections.blurb: What the AI build-out draws from the National Electricity Market — the binding constraint on the whole sector.

tiles.label: Data centre electricity use, FY2024–25
tiles.context: ≈2% of all electricity in the NEM today.

tiles.label: Forecast demand by 2030
tiles.context: AEMO expects data centre demand to triple by 2030.

tiles.label: Forecast demand by 2050
tiles.context: ≈12% of NEM power by 2049–50 under the Step Change scenario.

tiles.label: Forecast annual growth in consumption
tiles.context: Average yearly growth in data centre electricity use to FY2030.

tiles.label: 'Phantom demand' in connection requests
tiles.context: Share of early-stage grid connection requests expected never to materialise — read pipeline GW with this discount.

sections.title: Capacity & footprint
sections.blurb: Installed capacity, the pipeline behind it, and where it physically sits.

tiles.label: Occupied capacity, 2025
tiles.context: Built-out national capacity of ~2 GW is projected to reach ~9.5 GW by the early 2030s.

tiles.label: Data centres nationally
tiles.context: Sydney (91) and Melbourne (51) hold more than half the sites and ~80% of national capacity.

tiles.label: NSW development pipeline
tiles.context: 44 projects in the NSW pipeline as of Q1 2026 — the largest of any state.

tiles.label: Victoria development pipeline
tiles.context: At least 30 data centres in the Victorian pipeline; ~90 more nationally.

tiles.label: National vacancy rate
tiles.context: Melbourne is among the tightest markets globally at 4–5% — capacity is effectively pre-sold.

sections.title: Compute density
sections.blurb: Australia doesn't publish national GPU or memory (HBM/DRAM) stock figures — rack power density and MW are the tracked proxies for AI compute and memory demand.

tiles.label: AI / GPU rack power density
tiles.context: AI training racks draw 10–20× the power of traditional enterprise racks — the reason every MW figure on this page is exploding.

tiles.label: Operational AI-era capacity by 2035
tiles.context: Expected operational data centre capacity by 2035, up from ~0.3 GW of AI-class load in 2024–25.

sections.title: Water
sections.blurb: The other physical input — cooling water is becoming a licensing issue in Sydney first.

tiles.label: Sydney data centre water use
tiles.context: Current drinking-water draw; Sydney Water has single-site applications for up to 40 ML/day (16 Olympic pools).

tiles.label: Share of city water supply
tiles.context: Small today, but Sydney Water scenarios reach up to 25% of supply by 2035 without recycled-water requirements.

sections.title: Money
sections.blurb: What the build-out and the wider AI market are worth.

tiles.label: Data centre investment pipeline
tiles.context: ≈5.6% of one year's GDP; ~A$26bn of new investment needed for capacity to double by 2030.

tiles.label: Cost to commission capacity
tiles.context: Per-megawatt commissioning cost (Oxford Economics for AusNet); implies A$20bn+ for the near-term build alone.

tiles.label: IDA fast-track cohort
tiles.context: 15 data centre projects endorsed for prioritised federal approvals (27 Mar 2026).

tiles.label: Australian IT spending, 2026
tiles.context: Driven by the corporate rush into AI, cyber security and cloud.

tiles.label: Potential GDP contribution by 2030
tiles.context: Government projection for AI and automation, cited in the National AI Plan.

sections.title: Industry & adoption
sections.blurb: The demand side: who is building, hiring and using AI in Australia.

tiles.label: AI businesses in Australia
tiles.context: Domestic AI industry market size ≈A$2.6bn in 2026.

tiles.label: Australians using AI tools monthly
tiles.context: Q1 2026 — Australian consumer gen-AI adoption outpaces the US and UK.

tiles.label: Employers with AI-mentioning job ads
tiles.context: 43% of software-dev and data-analytics postings now mention AI — the highest of any occupation.

tiles.label: SMEs adopting AI
tiles.context: Small and medium enterprise adoption, Q1 2026.

tiles.label: Organisations using AI
tiles.context: Per the Austrade/CSIRO Australian AI Industry Capability Report.
